titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Hibbert Driver Papers
titlestmt.author: Abigail Kromminga

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 6, 2024 at 22:00

did.abstract: This collection contains materials relating to George Hibbert Driver's years in Palestine and Germany in 1905-1907, studying Christian theology abroad on a Hooker Fellowship after graduating from Yale Divinity School. The collection includes extensive photographs from Palestine created, organized, dated, and described by Driver, which can be read together with correspondence Driver sent to his parents in the United States. Driver's essays, letters, photographs, and captions provide insight into an American, Christian view of Palestine, ever filtered through his religious and Orientalizing expectation.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Carmen D. Valentino Rare Books and Manuscripts on the Kenneth Scott Latourette Fund, 2024.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Hibbert Driver Papers (RG 344), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: George Hibbert Driver was born in Lynn, Massachusetts, in 1879, and graduated from Amherst College in 1900 and 1904, and Yale Divinity School in 1905. In 1905, Driver traveled to Palestine on a Hooker Fellowship, where he studied at the American School of Oriental Study and Research. Founded in 1900 by Charles Cutler Torrey, the American School of Oriental Study and Research in Palestine was, in Driver's words, "a laboratory course in sacred geography." Located in Jerusalem, the school excavated sites across Palestine and Lebanon deemed to have biblical significance, beginning with the tombs of Sidon in 1900.

In 1905 and 1906, Driver traveled throughout Palestine, creating photographs and writing down his impressions, spiritual experiences, and comments on Ottoman rule. In April of 1906, Driver left Palestine to study at the theological faculties in Marburg and Berlin.

Upon returning to the United States in 1907, Driver began his work as a Congregational minister, serving in churches across the Northeast. He continued writing—publishing short stories, poetry, and monographs on the doctrine of the Holy Spirit. George Hibbert Driver died in Marblehead, Massachusetts, in 1966.

Further information on the American School of Oriental Study and Research in Palestine, can be found in the papers of Charles Cutler Torrey, Andover and Yale professor, RG 271 at Yale Divinity Library Special Collections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection concisely covers George Hibbert Driver's 1905-1906 trip to the American School of Oriental Study and Research in Palestine in correspondence, journalistic and diaristic essays, and photographs. Further correspondence covers Driver's time studying at the theological faculties in Marburg and Berlin. The collection provides insights into American, Christian concepts of "sacred geography" read onto late-Ottoman Palestine.

Series I, Correspondence, contains letters written by Driver addressed to his parents in the United States. The letters describe in depth his travels and studies. Further correspondence, sent to Driver, describes theological study in Germany, mentioning professors like Julius Wellhausen, and, in the correspondence from Edward H. Smith, American Christian missions in Fuzhou, China.

Series II, Writings, contains both published articles and drafts composed by Driver. In the writings, Driver provides an introduction to the American School ("Our Jerusalem Colony," "The American School for Oriental Study and Research at Home in Jerusalem"), describes his travel impressions, and comments on the politics of late-Ottoman rule ("Palestine and the Yemen Uprising"). Notably, an essay written later in 1918, "The Light on Jerusalem's Cross," addresses the British occupation of Palestine following WWI.

Series III, Collected materials, contains ephemera gathered from Driver's travels in Europe and the Middle East, writings by others and booklets, and compiled biographical info from genealogical websites.

Series IV, Photographs, is divided into two subseries. The first retains Driver's organization—the photographs are paired with negatives numerically labeled beginning with P, which according to Driver stands for "Palestine Trip." On each negative envelope, Driver describes the image, location, and impressions documented in the picture. Later numbers in the series are often dated, allowing the researcher to collate and contextualize the photographs with correspondence in Series I. The second subseries consists primarily of reprints, some of which have descriptions written on the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Edward H. Smith in Fuzhou China, Raymond G. Clapp in Goettingen regarding study in Germany, and Hans H. L.(?) regarding going to Palestine.

did.unittitle: GHD to family from ship and Rome
GHD to family from Sidon, Haifa, and Jerusalem
GHD to family from Jerusalem and Jericho
GHD to family from Cairo and Alexandria
GHD to family from Milan and Marburg
GHD to family from Marburg, Schwarzwald, Zurich, Geneva, Munich, and Eisenach
GHD to family from Dresden and Berlin
GHD to family from Berlin, Amsterdam, and Brussels
GHD to family from Paris, Normandy, and London
GHD to family from Cambridge, Glasgow, and Edinburgh
From a friend in Marburg, from GHD in Wakefield, Massachusetts
"Some Reminiscences of Egypt," "Letter from Berlin," Wakefield Citizen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes comments on German colonialism in Namibia (German South West Africa).

did.unittitle: "The Bridegroom of Gibeon," "The Greek Annual Wash in the Jordan"
"The American School for Oriental Study and Research At Home in Jerusalem"
"Little Bloomer Boys of Lebanon," "A Puzzle Picture from Jerusalem," "Child-life in Palestine"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written as children's stories.

did.unittitle: "A Sabbath at the Cedars of Lebanon"
"Palestine and the Yemen Uprising," "The Turkish Revolution and the Christian Outlook in the Near East"
"Farms in Palestine and Egypt," "Palestine and Egypt," "Palestine the Home of Our Lord"
"How I Meditated on the Mount Called Olivet: Little Glimpses of Palestine by a Yale Fellow at Jerusalem"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typscript essay including photographs pasted in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text celebrating the British occupation of Palestine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards sent from Beirut and Istanbul ("Constantinople").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an announcement of Edgar J. Banks lecture series, 1909, booklet "The American School of Oriental Research," 1923, and a handwritten undated essay on women in China perhaps by Shi Kung Tsao.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph prints and negatives numerically organized by GHD and labeled "P = Palestine Trip." Each negative envelope contains a written description of the image.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives from Italy, Gibralter, and Greece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Constantinople, Beirut, and Rayfoun, Lebanon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Lebanon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from GHD's trip to the Cedars of Lebanon and Beirut. For related materials, see also box 2, folder 1, "A Sabbath at the Cedars of Lebanon."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Sidon, Lebanon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from archaeological sites in Syria, Tyre, Acre, the Sea of Galilee, and Jezreel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Palestine, including Mount Hermonit ("Little Hermon"), Nazareth, Mount Carmel, Tell el-Mutesellim ("Megiddo").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Samaria, Schechem, the Latin Monastery in Jifna, Beitin ("Bethel"), An-Nabi Samwil ("Neby Samwil"), and photographs of a funeral procession in Arraba and school children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Jerusalem, including the Mount of Olives, Absalom's Tomb, Job's well, Isaiah's tree, and boys playing kickball.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Bethlehem, Nablus, and Jerusalem, including portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from the Friends Mission School, Khirbet Seilun ("Shiloh"), Bethoron, and Ramallah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Bethlehem, including Christmas processions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Wadi Qelt ("Wady Kelt"), Jericho, and the Dead Sea.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of baptisms in the Jordan River.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from "Frank Mountain," the bedouin community at Wadi Khureitun, and Tekoa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Jerusalem, 'Anata, Ein Farah, and Mar Saba Monastery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Jerusalem and Bethlehem, including portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Hebron, Artuf, Qalqilya ("Kalkilieh"), and Caesarea.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Nazareth, Tiberias, the Sea of Galilee, and Khirbet al-Minyeh ("Khan Minyeh").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, including from the Sea of Galilee and the Synagogue ruins in Kafr Bir'im.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograhs from Gadara.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Mount Gerezim and Shechem, portrait of Khalil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Jerusalem, including portraits of pilgrims, and Jaffa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Cairo.

did.unittitle: Labeled P355-P356, miscellaneous negatives, and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives from Venice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most of the following photographs are duplicate prints from the "P" series.

did.unittitle: From Sidon, the Dead Sea, Cedars of Lebanon, and unidentified

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some photographs include captions written by GHD.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs with captions written by GHD.

did.unittitle: Portraits and group photographs from Palestine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains small and cut image fragments.

did.unittitle: Photographs and negatives from onboard the S.S. Arabic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from correspondence with Aunt Sue in box 1, folder 12.
